title: NWE vs. WEC: Which Stock Is the Better Value Option?
text: Investors interested in Utility - Electric Power stocks are likely familiar with NorthWestern (NWE) and WEC Energy Group (WEC). But which of these two stocks is more attractive to value investors?

title: WEC Energy Group: Adversely Impacted By Warm Weather But Still A Good Utility
text: WEC Energy Group is a regulated utility serving four states in the northern Heartland, including the city of Chicago. The company's natural gas operations were adversely impacted by a warmer-than-normal winter, but its overall stability remains intact.

title: Top 4 Utilities Stocks That Could Lead To Your Biggest Gains In Q2 - Exelon (NASDAQ:EXC), American Electric Power (NASDAQ:AEP)
text: The most oversold stocks in the utilities sector presents an opportunity to buy into undervalued companies.

title: POR or WEC: Which Is the Better Value Stock Right Now?
text: Investors interested in stocks from the Utility - Electric Power sector have probably already heard of Portland General Electric (POR) and WEC Energy Group (WEC). But which of these two stocks is more attractive to value investors?

title: WEC Energy (WEC) Q1 Earnings Surpass Estimates, Sales Lag
text: WEC Energy Group's (WEC) first-quarter 2023 earnings beat estimates, while revenues miss the same.

title: WEC Energy Group (WEC) Q1 Earnings Beat Estimates
text: WEC Energy Group (WEC) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.61 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.59 per share. This compares to earnings of $1.79 per share a year ago.
